channel.title: Home on LocalGlobal
channel.description: Recent content in Home on LocalGlobal

item.title: About
item.description: Who I am, what I'm working on, and where this is headed.

item.title: Annotated Bibliography
item.description: Every paper, book, and resource informing the notation project, annotated by relevance.

item.title: Annotated Reading List
item.description: A phase-organised curriculum with honest annotations — what each text actually teaches, not what the publisher promises.

item.title: Colophon
item.description: How this site is built, what tools are used, and what design decisions were made and why.

item.title: Design Log
item.description: A chronological narrative of major design decisions, interesting failures, and evidence-driven changes.

item.title: Experiments
item.description: Empirical results from perceptual discriminability, error rate, derivation speed, and cross-domain transfer measurements.

item.title: How I Study Mathematics
item.description: The complete methodology: periodised schedule, error journal, synthesis journal, flashcard strategy, and competition mathematics integration.

item.title: Notation Specification
item.description: The living specification: radical vocabulary, domain markers, composition rules, and translation tables. Current version.

item.title: What Local Global Means
item.description:
Welcome to Local-Global!

This site is intended to explore ideas at the intersection of mathematics, philosophy, language, design, ecology, and human problem spaces.

Why “Local-Global”?

In creating a title for this site I was initially drawn towards phrases using mathematical language as a reflection of my academic specialization. However, I decided against this for one reason: The ideas I like to engage with tend to span across many disciplines, math being just one. The perspective I’m trying to adopt for this site and for my future research emphasizes understanding how local decisions, events, or objects interact with global systems, whether in mathematics or elsewhere. Hence, local-global.
